LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANTS RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES AN INADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW IN THAT THE PROPERTY IS UNIQUE, AND DEMONSTRATES IMMINENT AND IRREPARABLE HARM BECAUSE THE PROPERTY IS HIS FAMILY'S HOME, HE FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT SHOWS A PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANT PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT IT PURCHASED PLAINTIFF'S LOAN AND DEED OF TRUST IN DECEMBER 2009, IT HAS AN INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY, AND THE AUTHORITY TO FORECLOSE. THERE, QUIET TITLE CLAIM UNLIKELY TO SUCCEED. WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE UNLIKELY TO SUCCEED BECAUSE NO FORECLOSURE HAS OCCURRED. DEFENDANT PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT FFB DID COMPLY WITH 2923.5 REQUIREMENTS TO CONTACT THE BORROWER AND DISCUSS OPTIONS REGARDING FORECLOSURE. NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED REGARDING FRAUD, MISREPRESENTATION OR CONCEALMENT. NO EVIDENCE OR FACTS PRESENTED TO ESTABLISH EXISTENCE OF A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND PLAINTIFF, SOMETHING BEYOND A BORROWER-LENDER RELATIONSHIP. NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2010 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SEASIDE FINANCIAL CORPORATION, and, ONEWEST BANK, FSB DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar as moot, dismissal of entire action filed on April 8, 2010. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause to Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Oxana Kozlov, Esq. of IBCI Law Group, (650) 814-7708 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Nicholas A. Subias, Esq. of Allen Matkins (415) 273-8403, appearing for Defendants OneWest Bank, FSB and Seaside Financial Corp. and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Although plaintiff demonstrates an inadequate remedy at law in that the property is unique, and demonstrates imminent and irreparable harm because the property is his family's home, he fails to present evidence that shows a probability of success on the merits. Defendant presents evidence that it purchased plaintiff's loan and deed of trust in December 2009, it has an interest in the property, and the authority to foreclose. There, quiet title claim unlikely to succeed. Wrongful foreclosure unlikely to succeed because no foreclosure has occurred. Defendant presents evidence that FFB did comply with 2923.5 requirements to contact the borrower and discuss options regarding foreclosure. No evidence presented regarding fraud, misrepresentation or concealment. No evidence or facts presented to establish existence of a fiduciary relationship between defendant and plaintiff, something beyond a borrower-lender relationship. No evidence supports the unfair business practices cause of action. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
